v1.letter_excerpt: The patient has a history of a stutter but it returned after the accident. We ask that you reconsider the denial and cover the speech therapy as necessary treatment...

v3.letter_excerpt: The patient overcame the childhood stutter completely and had normal speech for years before the accident. The current dysfluency arose in direct temporal relation to the motor vehicle injury — making this a trauma-induced relapse requiring restorative therapy under Aetna's own policy language...

what_changed: Earlier drafts acknowledged the childhood history without clearly establishing the intervening period of normal speech that separates developmental from acquired dysfluency.
The improved version makes the pre-accident baseline explicit, which is the key distinction between developmental (excluded) and restorative (covered) therapy.
